Creative is your currency during BFCM. With higher ad costs and tighter competition, only the best assets cut through.

In this episode, Nick breaks down exactly how to plan, produce, and launch high-performing creative for every phase - from early teasers to post-sale UGC.

Simple > clever. Reuse what works. Scale what converts.

🎥 Full unmissable episode now on our YouTube [link in bio]

It's October 23rd, here's Nick's email gameplan for the next 10 days:

📬 𝟭. 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻
Don’t start with a sale, start with a story. Warm up your audience before you pitch.

🎃 𝟮. 𝗧𝗮𝗽 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗼 𝗰𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗺𝗼𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀
Use fun dates like Halloween to stay relevant and keep engagement high.

🧠 𝟯. 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝘆 𝘁𝗼𝗽 𝗼𝗳 𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗱
When you finally reveal your offer in early November, your audience is already primed.

firstComment: What about optimizing for value instead of lowest cost

latestComments.text: What about optimizing for value instead of lowest cost

Most brands blame Meta for auction fatigue when the real bottleneck is creative homogeneity signaling the same customer profile repeatedly which caps spend ceiling because algorithms optimize for conversion likelihood at specific price points meaning your single hero ad just taught the platform to only find your cheapest buyers.

Great point—thinking of creative strategy as a data problem really reframes how teams approach performance marketing. When creative decisions are guided by deeper audience insights and performance signals, campaigns move beyond competing for the same low-value customers and start attracting higher-intent audiences that actually drive scalable growth. Do you think most brands today have the right data infrastructure to truly inform their creative strategy, or are many still optimizing mostly on instinct?

caption: *misses call entirely*

Every time content pauses, revenue dips.
I have seen it.
Nick’s seen this across his companies and every brand he touches. 🧠
Posting consistently isn’t optional, it’s the advantage. 🎯

latestComments.text:
The organic-to-paid synergy is real - your organic activity essentially "trains" the algorithm on your audience preferences and engagement patterns, while inactive accounts miss out on those crucial signals that make paid campaigns more effective.

Have you noticed specific types of organic content that seem to boost your paid performance more than others?

Your countdown starts now.

We’ve teamed up with Nick Shackelford to drop daily tactics that’ll help you nail your BFCM prep — from ad creative to campaign timing to cash flow.

🎥 Episode 1 drops Oct 16th — kicking off with your perfect 50-day calendar to make this your biggest BFCM yet.

caption: Happiest birthday to our guy. The leader of our morning dance parties. Kings and I would be so bored without you!🤎

He’s spent years behind the scenes powering 8-9 figure brands — now he’s building his own and redefining customer acquisition from the inside out. 🚀
Episode 8 features @iamnickshackelford , Partner at @drinkabrez and a performance marketing heavyweight who’s spent the last decade scaling brands with creative-first strategy and real operator reps.
We get into:
• Why creative is the new targeting—and what Meta’s new algorithm actually rewards
• Nick’s ideal acquisition framework: how to balance proven winners vs. testing
• Conversion rate optimization strategies that immediately impact performance
• How BREZ cut churn from 24% to 17%—and what it’ll take to hit sub-5%
• Lessons from scaling Konstant Creative + Structured Agency
🎤 Episode drops this Thursday.
Follow @openresidency for more 🧠

firstComment: That first line is everything. Most D2C founders can describe their revenue trajectory but can't trace which levers actually moved it. The dashboard isn't the fix — it's the discipline of asking the right questions before building it.

latestComments.text: That first line is everything. Most D2C founders can describe their revenue trajectory but can't trace which levers actually moved it. The dashboard isn't the fix — it's the discipline of asking the right questions before building it.
This looks like a useful tool to test.

Exactamente 💡 En LATAM muchos emprendedores tienen el negocio "de memoria" pero sin datos reales. La diferencia entre crecer y estancarse está en tener métricas claras al alcance. El que domina sus números, domina su crecimiento — y eso empieza con las herramientas correctas desde el día 1.
"Can't explain your numbers, you don't own your growth" — this hit hard. Building @weareshotzy from Vizag and we learned this the tough way. Tracking what matters changed everything for us 📊🔥
